content_markdown:
A source produces water waves of frequency $10\text{ Hz}$. The graph shows the variation with horizontal position of the vertical displacement of the surface of water at one instant in time.

The speed of the water waves is

diagram_metadata.description: A displacement-position graph showing a sinusoidal wave. The vertical axis is 'vertical displacement / cm' with peaks at 0.4 and troughs at -0.4. The horizontal axis is 'horizontal position / cm' with markings at 0, 1.0, 2.0, 3.0, and 4.0. One full wave cycle is completed between 0 and 2.0 cm.

vibe_explanation: To find the wave speed, use the wave equation $v = f\lambda$. From the graph, we can see that one full wavelength $\lambda$ corresponds to the distance between $0$ and $2.0\text{ cm}$, so $\lambda = 2.0\text{ cm}$. Given the frequency $f = 10\text{ Hz}$, the speed is $v = 10\text{ Hz} \times 2.0\text{ cm} = 20\text{ cm s}^{-1}$.
